<a id="service-com-daml-ledger-api-v2-commandcompletionservice"></a>
### Service `CommandCompletionService`

- Lifecycle state: -
- Replaces: -
- Source: [community/ledger-api/src/main/protobuf/com/daml/ledger/api/v2/command_completion_service.proto](https://github.com/DACH-NY/canton/blob/v3.4.11/community/ledger-api/src/main/protobuf/com/daml/ledger/api/v2/command_completion_service.proto#L29)
- Endpoints tracked: `1`

Allows clients to observe the status of their submissions.
 Commands may be submitted via the Command Submission Service.
 The on-ledger effects of their submissions are disclosed by the Update Service.

 Commands may fail in 2 distinct manners:

 1. Failure communicated synchronously in the gRPC error of the submission.
 2. Failure communicated asynchronously in a Completion, see ``completion.proto``.

 Note that not only successfully submitted commands MAY produce a completion event. For example, the participant MAY
 choose to produce a completion event for a rejection of a duplicate command.

 Clients that do not receive a successful completion about their submission MUST NOT assume that it was successful.
 Clients SHOULD subscribe to the CompletionStream before starting to submit commands to prevent race conditions.

<a id="service-com-daml-ledger-api-v2-commandservice"></a>
### Service `CommandService`

- Lifecycle state: -
- Replaces: -
- Source: [community/ledger-api/src/main/protobuf/com/daml/ledger/api/v2/command_service.proto](https://github.com/DACH-NY/canton/blob/v3.4.11/community/ledger-api/src/main/protobuf/com/daml/ledger/api/v2/command_service.proto#L23)
- Endpoints tracked: `3`

Command Service is able to correlate submitted commands with completion data, identify timeouts, and return contextual
 information with each tracking result. This supports the implementation of stateless clients.

 Note that submitted commands generally produce completion events as well, even in case a command gets rejected.
 For example, the participant SHOULD produce a completion event for a rejection of a duplicate command.

Allows clients to attempt advancing the ledger's state by submitting commands.
 The final states of their submissions are disclosed by the Command Completion Service.
 The on-ledger effects of their submissions are disclosed by the Update Service.

 Commands may fail in 2 distinct manners:

 1. Failure communicated synchronously in the gRPC error of the submission.
 2. Failure communicated asynchronously in a Completion, see ``completion.proto``.

 Note that not only successfully submitted commands MAY produce a completion event. For example, the participant MAY
 choose to produce a completion event for a rejection of a duplicate command.

 Clients that do not receive a successful completion about their submission MUST NOT assume that it was successful.
 Clients SHOULD subscribe to the CompletionStream before starting to submit commands to prevent race conditions.

**Signature**

```protobuf
rpc ContractService.GetContract(com.daml.ledger.api.v2.GetContractRequest) returns (com.daml.ledger.api.v2.GetContractResponse);
```

Looking up contract data by contract ID.
 This endpoint is experimental / alpha, therefore no backwards compatibility is guaranteed.
 This endpoint must not be used to look up contracts which entered the participant via party replication
 or repair service.
 If there is no contract exist with the contract ID, or there is no intersection with the querying_parties,
 an CONTRACT_PAYLOAD_NOT_FOUND error will be raised.

```protobuf

```

Get the create and the consuming exercise event for the contract with the provided ID.
 No events will be returned for contracts that have been pruned because they
 have already been archived before the latest pruning offset.
 If the contract cannot be found for the request, or all the contract-events are filtered, a CONTRACT_EVENTS_NOT_FOUND error will be raised.

<a id="endpoint-com-daml-ledger-api-v2-stateservice-getactivecontracts"></a>
**Endpoint `StateService.GetActiveContracts`**

- Lifecycle state: -
- Replaces: -
- Introduced in: `3.4.0`
- Last changed in: `3.4.0`
- Removed in: `-`
- Status: `current`
- Source: [community/ledger-api/src/main/protobuf/com/daml/ledger/api/v2/state_service.proto](https://github.com/DACH-NY/canton/blob/v3.4.11/community/ledger-api/src/main/protobuf/com/daml/ledger/api/v2/state_service.proto#L23)

**Signature**

```protobuf
rpc StateService.GetActiveContracts(com.daml.ledger.api.v2.GetActiveContractsRequest) returns (stream com.daml.ledger.api.v2.GetActiveContractsResponse);
```

Returns a stream of the snapshot of the active contracts and incomplete (un)assignments at a ledger offset.
 Once the stream of GetActiveContractsResponses completes,
 the client SHOULD begin streaming updates from the update service,
 starting at the GetActiveContractsRequest.active_at_offset specified in this request.
 Clients SHOULD NOT assume that the set of active contracts they receive reflects the state at the ledger end.

**History**

| Version | Kind | Details |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `3.4.0` | `introduced` |  |

**Request Type**

- [`com.daml.ledger.api.v2.GetActiveContractsRequest`](#type-com-daml-ledger-api-v2-getactivecontractsrequest)

**Response Type**

- [`com.daml.ledger.api.v2.GetActiveContractsResponse`](#type-com-daml-ledger-api-v2-getactivecontractsresponse)

<a id="service-com-daml-ledger-api-v2-updateservice"></a>
### Service `UpdateService`

- Lifecycle state: -
- Replaces: -
- Source: [community/ledger-api/src/main/protobuf/com/daml/ledger/api/v2/update_service.proto](https://github.com/DACH-NY/canton/blob/v3.4.11/community/ledger-api/src/main/protobuf/com/daml/ledger/api/v2/update_service.proto#L30)
- Endpoints tracked: `3`

Allows clients to read updates (transactions, (un)assignments, topology events) from the ledger.

 ``GetUpdates`` and ``GetUpdateTrees`` provide a comprehensive stream of updates/changes
 which happened on the virtual shared ledger. These streams are indexed with ledger
 offsets, which are strictly increasing.
 The virtual shared ledger consist of changes happening on multiple synchronizers which are
 connected to the serving participant. Each update belongs to one synchronizer, this is
 provided in the result (the ``synchronizer_id`` field in ``Transaction``
 for transactions, the ``source`` field in ``UnassignedEvent`` and the ``target`` field in ``AssignedEvent``).
 Consumers can rely on strong causal guarantees on the virtual shared ledger for a single
 synchronizer: updates which have greater offsets are happened after than updates with smaller
 offsets for the same synchronizer. Across different synchronizers this is not guaranteed.

<a id="endpoint-com-daml-ledger-api-v2-updateservice-getupdates"></a>
**Endpoint `UpdateService.GetUpdates`**

- Lifecycle state: -
- Replaces: -
- Introduced in: `3.4.0`
- Last changed in: `3.4.0`
- Removed in: `-`
- Status: `current`
- Source: [community/ledger-api/src/main/protobuf/com/daml/ledger/api/v2/update_service.proto](https://github.com/DACH-NY/canton/blob/v3.4.11/community/ledger-api/src/main/protobuf/com/daml/ledger/api/v2/update_service.proto#L37)

**Signature**

```protobuf
rpc UpdateService.GetUpdates(com.daml.ledger.api.v2.GetUpdatesRequest) returns (stream com.daml.ledger.api.v2.GetUpdatesResponse);
```

Read the ledger's filtered update stream for the specified contents and filters.
 It returns the event types in accordance with the stream contents selected. Also the selection criteria
 for individual events depends on the transaction shape chosen.

 - ACS delta: a requesting party must be a stakeholder of an event for it to be included.
 - ledger effects: a requesting party must be a witness of an en event for it to be included.

**History**

| Version | Kind | Details |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `3.4.0` | `introduced` |  |

- [`com.daml.ledger.api.v2.GetUpdatesRequest`](#type-com-daml-ledger-api-v2-getupdatesrequest)

**Response Type**

- [`com.daml.ledger.api.v2.GetUpdatesResponse`](#type-com-daml-ledger-api-v2-getupdatesresponse)

In particular, the last offset, node_id pair is preserved.<br/> The last update is the most recent update created or assigned this contract on synchronizer_id synchronizer.<br/> The offset of the CreatedEvent might point to an already pruned update, therefore it cannot necessarily be used<br/> for lookups. |

This strictly increases<br/> with each unassign command for the same contract. Creation of the contract corresponds to reassignment_counter<br/> equals zero.<br/> This field will be the reassignment_counter of the latest observable activation event on this synchronizer, which is<br/> before the active_at_offset.<br/> Required |

<a id="type-com-daml-ledger-api-v2-archivedevent"></a>
**Message `com.daml.ledger.api.v2.ArchivedEvent`**

- Source: [community/ledger-api/src/main/protobuf/com/daml/ledger/api/v2/event.proto](https://github.com/DACH-NY/canton/blob/v3.4.11/community/ledger-api/src/main/protobuf/com/daml/ledger/api/v2/event.proto#L171)
- Fields: 7

Records that a contract has been archived, and choices may no longer be exercised on it.

| Field | Type | Label | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| offset | `int64` | optional | The offset of origin.<br/> Offsets are managed by the participant nodes.<br/> Transactions can thus NOT be assumed to have the same offsets on different participant nodes.<br/> Required, it is a valid absolute offset (positive integer) |
| node_id | `int32` | optional | The position of this event in the originating transaction or reassignment.<br/> Node IDs are not necessarily equal across participants,<br/> as these may see different projections/parts of transactions.<br/> Required, must be valid node ID (non-negative integer) |
| contract_id | `string` | optional | The ID of the archived contract.<br/> Must be a valid LedgerString (as described in ``value.proto``).<br/> Required |
| template_id | `com.daml.ledger.api.v2.Identifier` | optional | Identifies the template that defines the choice that archived the contract.<br/> This template's package-id may differ from the target contract's package-id<br/> if the target contract has been upgraded or downgraded.<br/><br/> The identifier uses the package-id reference format.<br/><br/> Required |
| witness_parties | `string` | repeated | The parties that are notified of this event. For an ``ArchivedEvent``,<br/> these are the intersection of the stakeholders of the contract in<br/> question and the parties specified in the ``TransactionFilter``. The<br/> stakeholders are the union of the signatories and the observers of<br/> the contract.<br/> Each one of its elements must be a valid PartyIdString (as described<br/> in ``value.proto``).<br/> Required |
| package_name | `string` | optional | The package name of the contract.<br/> Required |
| implemented_interfaces | `com.daml.ledger.api.v2.Identifier` | repeated | The interfaces implemented by the target template that have been<br/> matched from the interface filter query.<br/> Populated only in case interface filters with include_interface_view set.<br/><br/> If defined, the identifier uses the package-id reference format.<br/><br/> Optional |

<a id="type-com-daml-ledger-api-v2-commands"></a>
**Message `com.daml.ledger.api.v2.Commands`**

- Source: [community/ledger-api/src/main/protobuf/com/daml/ledger/api/v2/commands.proto](https://github.com/DACH-NY/canton/blob/v3.4.11/community/ledger-api/src/main/protobuf/com/daml/ledger/api/v2/commands.proto#L138)
- Fields: 15

A composite command that groups multiple commands together.

| Field | Type | Label | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| workflow_id | `string` | optional | Identifier of the on-ledger workflow that this command is a part of.<br/> Must be a valid LedgerString (as described in ``value.proto``).<br/> Optional |
| user_id | `string` | optional | Uniquely identifies the participant user that issued the command.<br/> Must be a valid UserIdString (as described in ``value.proto``).<br/> Required unless authentication is used with a user token.<br/> In that case, the token's user-id will be used for the request's user_id. |
| command_id | `string` | optional | Uniquely identifies the command.<br/> The triple (user_id, act_as, command_id) constitutes the change ID for the intended ledger change,<br/> where act_as is interpreted as a set of party names.<br/> The change ID can be used for matching the intended ledger changes with all their completions.<br/> Must be a valid LedgerString (as described in ``value.proto``).<br/> Required |
| commands | [`com.daml.ledger.api.v2.Command`](#type-com-daml-ledger-api-v2-command) | repeated | Individual elements of this atomic command. Must be non-empty.<br/> Required |
| deduplication_duration | `google.protobuf.Duration` | optional | Specifies the length of the deduplication period.<br/> It is interpreted relative to the local clock at some point during the submission's processing.<br/> Must be non-negative. Must not exceed the maximum deduplication time. |
| deduplication_offset | `int64` | optional | Specifies the start of the deduplication period by a completion stream offset (exclusive).<br/> Must be a valid absolute offset (positive integer) or participant begin (zero). |
| min_ledger_time_abs | `google.protobuf.Timestamp` | optional | Lower bound for the ledger time assigned to the resulting transaction.<br/> Note: The ledger time of a transaction is assigned as part of command interpretation.<br/> Use this property if you expect that command interpretation will take a considerate amount of time, such that by<br/> the time the resulting transaction is sequenced, its assigned ledger time is not valid anymore.<br/> Must not be set at the same time as min_ledger_time_rel.<br/> Optional |
| min_ledger_time_rel | `google.protobuf.Duration` | optional | Same as min_ledger_time_abs, but specified as a duration, starting from the time the command is received by the server.<br/> Must not be set at the same time as min_ledger_time_abs.<br/> Optional |
| act_as | `string` | repeated | Set of parties on whose behalf the command should be executed.<br/> If ledger API authorization is enabled, then the authorization metadata must authorize the sender of the request<br/> to act on behalf of each of the given parties.<br/> Each element must be a valid PartyIdString (as described in ``value.proto``).<br/> Required, must be non-empty. |
| read_as | `string` | repeated | Set of parties on whose behalf (in addition to all parties listed in ``act_as``) contracts can be retrieved.<br/> This affects Daml operations such as ``fetch``, ``fetchByKey``, ``lookupByKey``, ``exercise``, and ``exerciseByKey``.<br/> Note: A participant node of a Daml network can host multiple parties. Each contract present on the participant<br/> node is only visible to a subset of these parties. A command can only use contracts that are visible to at least<br/> one of the parties in ``act_as`` or ``read_as``. This visibility check is independent from the Daml authorization<br/> rules for fetch operations.<br/> If ledger API authorization is enabled, then the authorization metadata must authorize the sender of the request<br/> to read contract data on behalf of each of the given parties.<br/> Optional |
| submission_id | `string` | optional | A unique identifier to distinguish completions for different submissions with the same change ID.<br/> Typically a random UUID. Applications are expected to use a different UUID for each retry of a submission<br/> with the same change ID.<br/> Must be a valid LedgerString (as described in ``value.proto``).<br/><br/> If omitted, the participant or the committer may set a value of their choice.<br/> Optional |
| disclosed_contracts | [`com.daml.ledger.api.v2.DisclosedContract`](#type-com-daml-ledger-api-v2-disclosedcontract) | repeated | Additional contracts used to resolve contract &amp; contract key lookups.<br/> Optional |
| synchronizer_id | `string` | optional | Must be a valid synchronizer id<br/> Optional |
| package_id_selection_preference | `string` | repeated | The package-id selection preference of the client for resolving<br/> package names and interface instances in command submission and interpretation |
| prefetch_contract_keys | [`com.daml.ledger.api.v2.PrefetchContractKey`](#type-com-daml-ledger-api-v2-prefetchcontractkey) | repeated | Fetches the contract keys into the caches to speed up the command processing.<br/> Should only contain contract keys that are expected to be resolved during interpretation of the commands.<br/> Keys of disclosed contracts do not need prefetching.<br/><br/> Optional |

<a id="type-com-daml-ledger-api-v2-completion"></a>
**Message `com.daml.ledger.api.v2.Completion`**

- Source: [community/ledger-api/src/main/protobuf/com/daml/ledger/api/v2/completion.proto](https://github.com/DACH-NY/canton/blob/v3.4.11/community/ledger-api/src/main/protobuf/com/daml/ledger/api/v2/completion.proto#L18)
- Fields: 11

A completion represents the status of a submitted command on the ledger: it can be successful or failed.

| Field | Type | Label | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| command_id | `string` | optional | The ID of the succeeded or failed command.<br/> Must be a valid LedgerString (as described in ``value.proto``).<br/> Required |
| status | `google.rpc.Status` | optional | Identifies the exact type of the error.<br/> It uses the same format of conveying error details as it is used for the RPC responses of the APIs.<br/> Optional |
| update_id | `string` | optional | The update_id of the transaction or reassignment that resulted from the command with command_id.<br/> Only set for successfully executed commands.<br/> Must be a valid LedgerString (as described in ``value.proto``). |
| user_id | `string` | optional | The user-id that was used for the submission, as described in ``commands.proto``.<br/> Must be a valid UserIdString (as described in ``value.proto``).<br/> Optional for historic completions where this data is not available. |
| act_as | `string` | repeated | The set of parties on whose behalf the commands were executed.<br/> Contains the ``act_as`` parties from ``commands.proto``<br/> filtered to the requesting parties in CompletionStreamRequest.<br/> The order of the parties need not be the same as in the submission.<br/> Each element must be a valid PartyIdString (as described in ``value.proto``).<br/> Optional for historic completions where this data is not available. |
| submission_id | `string` | optional | The submission ID this completion refers to, as described in ``commands.proto``.<br/> Must be a valid LedgerString (as described in ``value.proto``).<br/> Optional |
| deduplication_offset | `int64` | optional | Specifies the start of the deduplication period by a completion stream offset (exclusive).<br/><br/> Must be a valid absolute offset (positive integer) or participant begin (zero). |
| deduplication_duration | `google.protobuf.Duration` | optional | Specifies the length of the deduplication period.<br/> It is measured in record time of completions.<br/><br/> Must be non-negative. |
| trace_context | [`com.daml.ledger.api.v2.TraceContext`](#type-com-daml-ledger-api-v2-tracecontext) | optional | Optional; ledger API trace context<br/><br/> The trace context transported in this message corresponds to the trace context supplied<br/> by the client application in a HTTP2 header of the original command submission.<br/> We typically use a header to transfer this type of information. Here we use message<br/> body, because it is used in gRPC streams which do not support per message headers.<br/> This field will be populated with the trace context contained in the original submission.<br/> If that was not provided, a unique ledger-api-server generated trace context will be used<br/> instead. |
| offset | `int64` | optional | May be used in a subsequent CompletionStreamRequest to resume the consumption of this stream at a later time.<br/> Required, must be a valid absolute offset (positive integer). |
| synchronizer_time | [`com.daml.ledger.api.v2.SynchronizerTime`](#type-com-daml-ledger-api-v2-synchronizertime) | optional | The synchronizer along with its record time.<br/> The synchronizer id provided, in case of<br/><br/> - successful/failed transactions: identifies the synchronizer of the transaction<br/> - for successful/failed unassign commands: identifies the source synchronizer<br/> - for successful/failed assign commands: identifies the target synchronizer<br/><br/> Required |

<a id="type-com-daml-ledger-api-v2-completionstreamrequest"></a>
**Message `com.daml.ledger.api.v2.CompletionStreamRequest`**

_No description._

| Field | Type | Label | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| user_id | `string` | optional | Only completions of commands submitted with the same user_id will be visible in the stream.<br/> Must be a valid UserIdString (as described in ``value.proto``).<br/> Required unless authentication is used with a user token.<br/> In that case, the token's user-id will be used for the request's user_id. |
| parties | `string` | repeated | Non-empty list of parties whose data should be included.<br/> The stream shows only completions of commands for which at least one of the ``act_as`` parties is in the given set of parties.<br/> Must be a valid PartyIdString (as described in ``value.proto``).<br/> Required |
| begin_exclusive | `int64` | optional | This optional field indicates the minimum offset for completions. This can be used to resume an earlier completion stream.<br/> If not set the ledger uses the ledger begin offset instead.<br/> If specified, it must be a valid absolute offset (positive integer) or zero (ledger begin offset).<br/> If the ledger has been pruned, this parameter must be specified and greater than the pruning offset. |

<a id="type-com-daml-ledger-api-v2-createdevent"></a>
**Message `com.daml.ledger.api.v2.CreatedEvent`**

- Source: [community/ledger-api/src/main/protobuf/com/daml/ledger/api/v2/event.proto](https://github.com/DACH-NY/canton/blob/v3.4.11/community/ledger-api/src/main/protobuf/com/daml/ledger/api/v2/event.proto#L37)
- Fields: 15

Records that a contract has been created, and choices may now be exercised on it.

| Field | Type | Label | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| offset | `int64` | optional | The offset of origin, which has contextual meaning, please see description at messages that include a CreatedEvent.<br/> Offsets are managed by the participant nodes.<br/> Transactions can thus NOT be assumed to have the same offsets on different participant nodes.<br/> Required, it is a valid absolute offset (positive integer) |
| node_id | `int32` | optional | The position of this event in the originating transaction or reassignment.<br/> The origin has contextual meaning, please see description at messages that include a CreatedEvent.<br/> Node IDs are not necessarily equal across participants,<br/> as these may see different projections/parts of transactions.<br/> Required, must be valid node ID (non-negative integer) |
| contract_id | `string` | optional | The ID of the created contract.<br/> Must be a valid LedgerString (as described in ``value.proto``).<br/> Required |
| template_id | `com.daml.ledger.api.v2.Identifier` | optional | The template of the created contract.<br/> The identifier uses the package-id reference format.<br/><br/> Required |
| contract_key | `com.daml.ledger.api.v2.Value` | optional | The key of the created contract.<br/> This will be set if and only if ``template_id`` defines a contract key.<br/> Optional |
| create_arguments | `com.daml.ledger.api.v2.Record` | optional | The arguments that have been used to create the contract.<br/><br/> Required |
| created_event_blob | `bytes` | optional | Opaque representation of contract create event payload intended for forwarding<br/> to an API server as a contract disclosed as part of a command<br/> submission.<br/> Optional |
| interface_views | [`com.daml.ledger.api.v2.InterfaceView`](#type-com-daml-ledger-api-v2-interfaceview) | repeated | Interface views specified in the transaction filter.<br/> Includes an ``InterfaceView`` for each interface for which there is a ``InterfaceFilter`` with<br/><br/> - its party in the ``witness_parties`` of this event,<br/> - and which is implemented by the template of this event,<br/> - and which has ``include_interface_view`` set.<br/><br/> Optional |
| witness_parties | `string` | repeated | The parties that are notified of this event. When a ``CreatedEvent``<br/> is returned as part of a transaction tree or ledger-effects transaction, this will include all<br/> the parties specified in the ``TransactionFilter`` that are witnesses  of the event<br/> (the stakeholders of the contract and all informees of all the ancestors<br/> of this create action that this participant knows about).<br/> If served as part of a ACS delta transaction those will<br/> be limited to all parties specified in the ``TransactionFilter`` that<br/> are stakeholders of the contract (i.e. either signatories or observers).<br/> If the ``CreatedEvent`` is returned as part of an AssignedEvent,<br/> ActiveContract or IncompleteUnassigned (so the event is related to<br/> an assignment or unassignment): this will include all parties of the<br/> ``TransactionFilter`` that are stakeholders of the contract.<br/><br/> The behavior of reading create events visible to parties not hosted<br/> on the participant node serving the Ledger API is undefined. Concretely,<br/> there is neither a guarantee that the participant node will serve all their<br/> create events on the ACS stream, nor is there a guarantee that matching archive<br/> events are delivered for such create events.<br/><br/> For most clients this is not a problem, as they only read events for parties<br/> that are hosted on the participant node. If you need to read events<br/> for parties that may not be hosted at all times on the participant node,<br/> subscribe to the ``TopologyEvent``s for that party by setting a corresponding<br/> ``UpdateFormat``.  Using these events, query the ACS as-of an offset where the<br/> party is hosted on the participant node, and ignore create events at offsets<br/> where the party is not hosted on the participant node.<br/> Required |
| signatories | `string` | repeated | The signatories for this contract as specified by the template.<br/> Required |
| observers | `string` | repeated | The observers for this contract as specified explicitly by the template or implicitly as choice controllers.<br/> This field never contains parties that are signatories.<br/> Required |
| created_at | `google.protobuf.Timestamp` | optional | Ledger effective time of the transaction that created the contract.<br/> Required |
| package_name | `string` | optional | The package name of the created contract.<br/> Required |
| acs_delta | `bool` | optional | Whether this event would be part of respective ACS_DELTA shaped stream,<br/> and should therefore considered when tracking contract activeness on the client-side.<br/> Required |
| representative_package_id | `string` | optional | A package-id present in the participant package store that typechecks the contract's argument.<br/> This may differ from the package-id of the template used to create the contract.<br/> For contracts created before Canton 3.4, this field matches the contract's creation package-id.<br/><br/> NOTE: Experimental, server internal concept, not for client consumption. Subject to change without notice.<br/><br/> Required |

A filter that matches all contracts that are either an instance of one of
 the ``template_filters`` or that match one of the ``interface_filters``.

| Field | Type | Label | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| wildcard_filter | [`com.daml.ledger.api.v2.WildcardFilter`](#type-com-daml-ledger-api-v2-wildcardfilter) | optional | A wildcard filter that matches all templates<br/> Optional |
| interface_filter | [`com.daml.ledger.api.v2.InterfaceFilter`](#type-com-daml-ledger-api-v2-interfacefilter) | optional | Include an ``InterfaceView`` for every ``InterfaceFilter`` matching a contract.<br/> The ``InterfaceFilter`` instances MUST each use a unique ``interface_id``.<br/> Optional |
| template_filter | [`com.daml.ledger.api.v2.TemplateFilter`](#type-com-daml-ledger-api-v2-templatefilter) | optional | A template for which the data will be included in the<br/> ``create_arguments`` of a matching ``CreatedEvent``.<br/> If a contract is simultaneously selected by a template filter and one or more interface filters,<br/> the corresponding ``include_created_event_blob`` are consolidated using an OR operation.<br/> Optional |

<a id="type-com-daml-ledger-api-v2-event"></a>
**Message `com.daml.ledger.api.v2.Event`**

- Source: [community/ledger-api/src/main/protobuf/com/daml/ledger/api/v2/event.proto](https://github.com/DACH-NY/canton/blob/v3.4.11/community/ledger-api/src/main/protobuf/com/daml/ledger/api/v2/event.proto#L26)
- Fields: 3

Events in transactions can have two primary shapes:

 - ACS delta: events can be CreatedEvent or ArchivedEvent
 - ledger effects: events can be CreatedEvent or ExercisedEvent

 In the update service the events are restricted to the events
 visible for the parties specified in the transaction filter. Each
 event message type below contains a ``witness_parties`` field which
 indicates the subset of the requested parties that can see the event
 in question.

| Field | Type | Label | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| created | [`com.daml.ledger.api.v2.CreatedEvent`](#type-com-daml-ledger-api-v2-createdevent) | optional | The event as it appeared in the context of its original daml transaction on this participant node.<br/> In particular, the offset, node_id pair of the daml transaction are preserved. |
| archived | [`com.daml.ledger.api.v2.ArchivedEvent`](#type-com-daml-ledger-api-v2-archivedevent) | optional |  |
| exercised | [`com.daml.ledger.api.v2.ExercisedEvent`](#type-com-daml-ledger-api-v2-exercisedevent) | optional |  |

<a id="type-com-daml-ledger-api-v2-eventformat"></a>
**Message `com.daml.ledger.api.v2.EventFormat`**

- Source: [community/ledger-api/src/main/protobuf/com/daml/ledger/api/v2/transaction_filter.proto](https://github.com/DACH-NY/canton/blob/v3.4.11/community/ledger-api/src/main/protobuf/com/daml/ledger/api/v2/transaction_filter.proto#L104)
- Fields: 3

A format for events which defines both which events should be included
 and what data should be computed and included for them.

 Note that some of the filtering behavior depends on the `TransactionShape`,
 which is expected to be specified alongside usages of `EventFormat`.

| Field | Type | Label | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| filters_by_party | `map<string, com.daml.ledger.api.v2.Filters>` | repeated | Each key must be a valid PartyIdString (as described in ``value.proto``).<br/> The interpretation of the filter depends on the transaction-shape being filtered:<br/><br/> 1. For **ledger-effects** create and exercise events are returned, for which the witnesses include at least one of<br/>    the listed parties and match the per-party filter.<br/> 2. For **transaction and active-contract-set streams** create and archive events are returned for all contracts whose<br/>    stakeholders include at least one of the listed parties and match the per-party filter.<br/><br/> Optional |
| filters_for_any_party | [`com.daml.ledger.api.v2.Filters`](#type-com-daml-ledger-api-v2-filters) | optional | Wildcard filters that apply to all the parties existing on the participant. The interpretation of the filters is the same<br/> with the per-party filter as described above.<br/> Optional |
| verbose | `bool` | optional | If enabled, values served over the API will contain more information than strictly necessary to interpret the data.<br/> In particular, setting the verbose flag to true triggers the ledger to include labels for record fields.<br/> Optional |

We plan to end support for this format in version 3.4.<br/><br/> Required |

Exercise a choice on an existing contract.

| Field | Type | Label | Description |

We plan to end support for this format in version 3.4.<br/> To exercise a choice on an interface, specify the interface identifier in the template_id field.<br/><br/> Required |

<a id="type-com-daml-ledger-api-v2-exercisedevent"></a>
**Message `com.daml.ledger.api.v2.ExercisedEvent`**

- Source: [community/ledger-api/src/main/protobuf/com/daml/ledger/api/v2/event.proto](https://github.com/DACH-NY/canton/blob/v3.4.11/community/ledger-api/src/main/protobuf/com/daml/ledger/api/v2/event.proto#L223)
- Fields: 15

Records that a choice has been exercised on a target contract.

| Field | Type | Label | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| offset | `int64` | optional | The offset of origin.<br/> Offsets are managed by the participant nodes.<br/> Transactions can thus NOT be assumed to have the same offsets on different participant nodes.<br/> Required, it is a valid absolute offset (positive integer) |
| node_id | `int32` | optional | The position of this event in the originating transaction or reassignment.<br/> Node IDs are not necessarily equal across participants,<br/> as these may see different projections/parts of transactions.<br/> Required, must be valid node ID (non-negative integer) |
| contract_id | `string` | optional | The ID of the target contract.<br/> Must be a valid LedgerString (as described in ``value.proto``).<br/> Required |
| template_id | `com.daml.ledger.api.v2.Identifier` | optional | Identifies the template that defines the executed choice.<br/> This template's package-id may differ from the target contract's package-id<br/> if the target contract has been upgraded or downgraded.<br/><br/> The identifier uses the package-id reference format.<br/><br/> Required |
| interface_id | `com.daml.ledger.api.v2.Identifier` | optional | The interface where the choice is defined, if inherited.<br/> If defined, the identifier uses the package-id reference format.<br/><br/> Optional |
| choice | `string` | optional | The choice that was exercised on the target contract.<br/> Must be a valid NameString (as described in ``value.proto``).<br/> Required |
| choice_argument | `com.daml.ledger.api.v2.Value` | optional | The argument of the exercised choice.<br/> Required |
| acting_parties | `string` | repeated | The parties that exercised the choice.<br/> Each element must be a valid PartyIdString (as described in ``value.proto``).<br/> Required |
| consuming | `bool` | optional | If true, the target contract may no longer be exercised.<br/> Required |
| witness_parties | `string` | repeated | The parties that are notified of this event. The witnesses of an exercise<br/> node will depend on whether the exercise was consuming or not.<br/> If consuming, the witnesses are the union of the stakeholders,<br/> the actors and all informees of all the ancestors of this event this<br/> participant knows about.<br/> If not consuming, the witnesses are the union of the signatories,<br/> the actors and all informees of all the ancestors of this event this<br/> participant knows about.<br/> In both cases the witnesses are limited to the querying parties, or not<br/> limited in case anyParty filters are used.<br/> Note that the actors might not necessarily be observers<br/> and thus stakeholders. This is the case when the controllers of a<br/> choice are specified using "flexible controllers", using the<br/> ``choice ... controller`` syntax, and said controllers are not<br/> explicitly marked as observers.<br/> Each element must be a valid PartyIdString (as described in ``value.proto``).<br/> Required |
| last_descendant_node_id | `int32` | optional | Specifies the upper boundary of the node ids of the events in the same transaction that appeared as a result of<br/> this ``ExercisedEvent``. This allows unambiguous identification of all the members of the subtree rooted at this<br/> node. A full subtree can be constructed when all descendant nodes are present in the stream. If nodes are heavily<br/> filtered, it is only possible to determine if a node is in a consequent subtree or not.<br/> Required |
| exercise_result | `com.daml.ledger.api.v2.Value` | optional | The result of exercising the choice.<br/> Required |
| package_name | `string` | optional | The package name of the contract.<br/> Required |
| implemented_interfaces | `com.daml.ledger.api.v2.Identifier` | repeated | If the event is consuming, the interfaces implemented by the target template that have been<br/> matched from the interface filter query.<br/> Populated only in case interface filters with include_interface_view set.<br/><br/> The identifier uses the package-id reference format.<br/><br/> Optional |
| acs_delta | `bool` | optional | Whether this event would be part of respective ACS_DELTA shaped stream,<br/> and should therefore considered when tracking contract activeness on the client-side.<br/> Required |

| Field | Type | Label | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| experimental | [`com.daml.ledger.api.v2.ExperimentalFeatures`](#type-com-daml-ledger-api-v2-experimentalfeatures) | optional | Features under development or features that are used<br/> for ledger implementation testing purposes only.<br/><br/> Daml applications SHOULD not depend on these in production. |
| user_management | [`com.daml.ledger.api.v2.UserManagementFeature`](#type-com-daml-ledger-api-v2-usermanagementfeature) | optional | If set, then the Ledger API server supports user management.<br/> It is recommended that clients query this field to gracefully adjust their behavior for<br/> ledgers that do not support user management. |
| party_management | [`com.daml.ledger.api.v2.PartyManagementFeature`](#type-com-daml-ledger-api-v2-partymanagementfeature) | optional | If set, then the Ledger API server supports party management configurability.<br/> It is recommended that clients query this field to gracefully adjust their behavior to<br/> maximum party page size. |
| offset_checkpoint | [`com.daml.ledger.api.v2.OffsetCheckpointFeature`](#type-com-daml-ledger-api-v2-offsetcheckpointfeature) | optional | It contains the timeouts related to the periodic offset checkpoint emission |
| package_feature | [`com.daml.ledger.api.v2.PackageFeature`](#type-com-daml-ledger-api-v2-packagefeature) | optional | If set, then the Ledger API server supports package listing<br/> configurability. It is recommended that clients query this field to<br/> gracefully adjust their behavior to maximum package listing page size. |

<a id="type-com-daml-ledger-api-v2-filters"></a>
**Message `com.daml.ledger.api.v2.Filters`**

- Source: [community/ledger-api/src/main/protobuf/com/daml/ledger/api/v2/transaction_filter.proto](https://github.com/DACH-NY/canton/blob/v3.4.11/community/ledger-api/src/main/protobuf/com/daml/ledger/api/v2/transaction_filter.proto#L15)
- Fields: 1

The union of a set of template filters, interface filters, or a wildcard.

| Field | Type | Label | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| cumulative | [`com.daml.ledger.api.v2.CumulativeFilter`](#type-com-daml-ledger-api-v2-cumulativefilter) | repeated | Every filter in the cumulative list expands the scope of the resulting stream. Each interface,<br/> template or wildcard filter means additional events that will match the query.<br/> The impact of include_interface_view and include_created_event_blob fields in the filters will<br/> also be accumulated.<br/> A template or an interface SHOULD NOT appear twice in the accumulative field.<br/> A wildcard filter SHOULD NOT be defined more than once in the accumulative field.<br/> Optional, if no ``CumulativeFilter`` defined, the default of a single ``WildcardFilter`` with<br/> include_created_event_blob unset is used. |

<a id="type-com-daml-ledger-api-v2-getactivecontractsrequest"></a>
**Message `com.daml.ledger.api.v2.GetActiveContractsRequest`**

- Source: [community/ledger-api/src/main/protobuf/com/daml/ledger/api/v2/state_service.proto](https://github.com/DACH-NY/canton/blob/v3.4.11/community/ledger-api/src/main/protobuf/com/daml/ledger/api/v2/state_service.proto#L40)
- Fields: 2

If the given offset is different than the ledger end, and there are (un)assignments in-flight at the given offset,
 the snapshot may fail with "FAILED_PRECONDITION/PARTICIPANT_PRUNED_DATA_ACCESSED".
 Note that it is ok to request acs snapshots for party migration with offsets other than ledger end, because party
 migration is not concerned with incomplete (un)assignments.

| Field | Type | Label | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| active_at_offset | `int64` | optional | The offset at which the snapshot of the active contracts will be computed.<br/> Must be no greater than the current ledger end offset.<br/> Must be greater than or equal to the last pruning offset.<br/> Required, must be a valid absolute offset (positive integer) or ledger begin offset (zero).<br/> If zero, the empty set will be returned. |
| event_format | [`com.daml.ledger.api.v2.EventFormat`](#type-com-daml-ledger-api-v2-eventformat) | optional | Format of the contract_entries in the result. In case of CreatedEvent the presentation will be of<br/> TRANSACTION_SHAPE_ACS_DELTA.<br/> Optional for backwards compatibility, defaults to an EventFormat where:<br/><br/> - filters_by_party is the filter.filters_by_party from this request<br/> - filters_for_any_party is the filter.filters_for_any_party from this request<br/> - verbose is the verbose field from this request |

<a id="type-com-daml-ledger-api-v2-getactivecontractsresponse"></a>
**Message `com.daml.ledger.api.v2.GetActiveContractsResponse`**

- Source: [community/ledger-api/src/main/protobuf/com/daml/ledger/api/v2/state_service.proto](https://github.com/DACH-NY/canton/blob/v3.4.11/community/ledger-api/src/main/protobuf/com/daml/ledger/api/v2/state_service.proto#L64)
- Fields: 4

_No description._

| Field | Type | Label | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| workflow_id | `string` | optional | The workflow ID used in command submission which corresponds to the contract_entry. Only set if<br/> the ``workflow_id`` for the command was set.<br/> Must be a valid LedgerString (as described in ``value.proto``).<br/> Optional |
| active_contract | [`com.daml.ledger.api.v2.ActiveContract`](#type-com-daml-ledger-api-v2-activecontract) | optional | The contract is active on the assigned synchronizer, meaning: there was an activation event on the given synchronizer (<br/> created, assigned), which is not followed by a deactivation event (archived, unassigned) on the same<br/> synchronizer, until the active_at_offset.<br/> Since activeness is defined as a per synchronizer concept, it is possible, that a contract is active on one<br/> synchronizer, but already archived on another.<br/> There will be one such message for each synchronizer the contract is active on. |
| incomplete_unassigned | [`com.daml.ledger.api.v2.IncompleteUnassigned`](#type-com-daml-ledger-api-v2-incompleteunassigned) | optional | Included iff the unassigned event was before or at the active_at_offset, but there was no corresponding<br/> assigned event before or at the active_at_offset. |
| incomplete_assigned | [`com.daml.ledger.api.v2.IncompleteAssigned`](#type-com-daml-ledger-api-v2-incompleteassigned) | optional | Important: this message is not indicating that the contract is active on the target synchronizer!<br/> Included iff the assigned event was before or at the active_at_offset, but there was no corresponding<br/> unassigned event before or at the active_at_offset. |

- Source: [community/ledger-api/src/main/protobuf/com/daml/ledger/api/v2/update_service.proto](https://github.com/DACH-NY/canton/blob/v3.4.11/community/ledger-api/src/main/protobuf/com/daml/ledger/api/v2/update_service.proto#L48)

| Field | Type | Label | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| begin_exclusive | `int64` | optional | Beginning of the requested ledger section (non-negative integer).<br/> The response will only contain transactions whose offset is strictly greater than this.<br/> If zero, the stream will start from the beginning of the ledger.<br/> If positive, the streaming will start after this absolute offset.<br/> If the ledger has been pruned, this parameter must be specified and be greater than the pruning offset. |
| end_inclusive | `int64` | optional | End of the requested ledger section.<br/> The response will only contain transactions whose offset is less than or equal to this.<br/> Optional, if empty, the stream will not terminate.<br/> If specified, the stream will terminate after this absolute offset (positive integer) is reached. |
| update_format | [`com.daml.ledger.api.v2.UpdateFormat`](#type-com-daml-ledger-api-v2-updateformat) | optional | Must be unset for GetUpdateTrees request.<br/> Optional for backwards compatibility for GetUpdates request: defaults to an UpdateFormat where:<br/><br/> - include_transactions.event_format.filters_by_party = the filter.filters_by_party on this request<br/> - include_transactions.event_format.filters_for_any_party = the filter.filters_for_any_party on this request<br/> - include_transactions.event_format.verbose = the same flag specified on this request<br/> - include_transactions.transaction_shape = TRANSACTION_SHAPE_ACS_DELTA<br/> - include_reassignments.filter = the same filter specified on this request<br/> - include_reassignments.verbose = the same flag specified on this request<br/> - include_topology_events.include_participant_authorization_events.parties = all the parties specified in filter |

**Message `com.daml.ledger.api.v2.ListVettedPackagesRequest`**

- Source: [community/ledger-api/src/main/protobuf/com/daml/ledger/api/v2/package_service.proto](https://github.com/DACH-NY/canton/blob/v3.4.11/community/ledger-api/src/main/protobuf/com/daml/ledger/api/v2/package_service.proto#L124)

| Field | Type | Label | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| package_metadata_filter | [`com.daml.ledger.api.v2.PackageMetadataFilter`](#type-com-daml-ledger-api-v2-packagemetadatafilter) | optional | The package metadata filter the returned vetted packages set must satisfy.<br/> Optional |
| topology_state_filter | [`com.daml.ledger.api.v2.TopologyStateFilter`](#type-com-daml-ledger-api-v2-topologystatefilter) | optional | The topology filter the returned vetted packages set must satisfy.<br/> Optional |
| page_token | `string` | optional | Pagination token to determine the specific page to fetch. Using the token<br/> guarantees that ``VettedPackages`` on a subsequent page are all greater<br/> (``VettedPackages`` are sorted by synchronizer ID then participant ID) than<br/> the last ``VettedPackages`` on a previous page.<br/><br/> The server does not store intermediate results between calls chained by a<br/> series of page tokens. As a consequence, if new vetted packages are being<br/> added and a page is requested twice using the same token, more packages can<br/> be returned on the second call.<br/><br/> Leave unspecified (i.e. as empty string) to fetch the first page.<br/><br/> Optional |
| page_size | `uint32` | optional | Maximum number of ``VettedPackages`` results to return in a single page.<br/><br/> If the page_size is unspecified (i.e. left as 0), the server will decide<br/> the number of results to be returned.<br/><br/> If the page_size exceeds the maximum supported by the server, an<br/> error will be returned.<br/><br/> To obtain the server's maximum consult the PackageService descriptor<br/> available in the VersionService.<br/><br/> Optional |

| next_page_token | `string` | optional | Pagination token to retrieve the next page.<br/> Empty string if there are no further results. |

<a id="type-com-daml-ledger-api-v2-packagemetadatafilter"></a>
**Message `com.daml.ledger.api.v2.PackageMetadataFilter`**

- Source: [community/ledger-api/src/main/protobuf/com/daml/ledger/api/v2/package_service.proto](https://github.com/DACH-NY/canton/blob/v3.4.11/community/ledger-api/src/main/protobuf/com/daml/ledger/api/v2/package_service.proto#L92)
- Fields: 2

Filter the VettedPackages by package metadata.

 A PackageMetadataFilter without package_ids and without package_name_prefixes
 matches any vetted package.

 Non-empty fields specify candidate values of which at least one must match.
 If both fields are set, then a candidate is returned if it matches one of the fields.

| Field | Type | Label | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| package_ids | `string` | repeated | If this list is non-empty, any vetted package with a package ID in this<br/> list will match the filter. |
| package_name_prefixes | `string` | repeated | If this list is non-empty, any vetted package with a name matching at least<br/> one prefix in this list will match the filter. |

<a id="type-com-daml-ledger-api-v2-reassignment"></a>
**Message `com.daml.ledger.api.v2.Reassignment`**

- Source: [community/ledger-api/src/main/protobuf/com/daml/ledger/api/v2/reassignment.proto](https://github.com/DACH-NY/canton/blob/v3.4.11/community/ledger-api/src/main/protobuf/com/daml/ledger/api/v2/reassignment.proto#L18)
- Fields: 8

Complete view of an on-ledger reassignment.

| Field | Type | Label | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| update_id | `string` | optional | Assigned by the server. Useful for correlating logs.<br/> Must be a valid LedgerString (as described in ``value.proto``).<br/> Required |
| command_id | `string` | optional | The ID of the command which resulted in this reassignment. Missing for everyone except the submitting party on the submitting participant.<br/> Must be a valid LedgerString (as described in ``value.proto``).<br/> Optional |
| workflow_id | `string` | optional | The workflow ID used in reassignment command submission. Only set if the ``workflow_id`` for the command was set.<br/> Must be a valid LedgerString (as described in ``value.proto``).<br/> Optional |
| offset | `int64` | optional | The participant's offset. The details of this field are described in ``community/ledger-api/README.md``.<br/> Required, must be a valid absolute offset (positive integer). |
| events | [`com.daml.ledger.api.v2.ReassignmentEvent`](#type-com-daml-ledger-api-v2-reassignmentevent) | repeated | The collection of reassignment events. Required. |
| trace_context | [`com.daml.ledger.api.v2.TraceContext`](#type-com-daml-ledger-api-v2-tracecontext) | optional | Optional; ledger API trace context<br/><br/> The trace context transported in this message corresponds to the trace context supplied<br/> by the client application in a HTTP2 header of the original command submission.<br/> We typically use a header to transfer this type of information. Here we use message<br/> body, because it is used in gRPC streams which do not support per message headers.<br/> This field will be populated with the trace context contained in the original submission.<br/> If that was not provided, a unique ledger-api-server generated trace context will be used<br/> instead. |
| record_time | `google.protobuf.Timestamp` | optional | The time at which the reassignment was recorded. The record time refers to the source/target<br/> synchronizer for an unassign/assign event respectively.<br/> Required |
| synchronizer_id | `string` | optional | A valid synchronizer id.<br/> Identifies the synchronizer that synchronized this Reassignment.<br/> Required |

| command_id | `string` | optional | Uniquely identifies the command.<br/> The triple (user_id, submitter, command_id) constitutes the change ID for the intended ledger change.<br/> The change ID can be used for matching the intended ledger changes with all their completions.<br/> Must be a valid LedgerString (as described in ``value.proto``).<br/> Required |
| submitter | `string` | optional | Party on whose behalf the command should be executed.<br/> If ledger API authorization is enabled, then the authorization metadata must authorize the sender of the request<br/> to act on behalf of the given party.<br/> Must be a valid PartyIdString (as described in ``value.proto``).<br/> Required |
| submission_id | `string` | optional | A unique identifier to distinguish completions for different submissions with the same change ID.<br/> Typically a random UUID. Applications are expected to use a different UUID for each retry of a submission<br/> with the same change ID.<br/> Must be a valid LedgerString (as described in ``value.proto``).<br/><br/> If omitted, the participant or the committer may set a value of their choice.<br/> Optional |
| commands | [`com.daml.ledger.api.v2.ReassignmentCommand`](#type-com-daml-ledger-api-v2-reassignmentcommand) | repeated | Individual elements of this reassignment. Must be non-empty. |

<a id="type-com-daml-ledger-api-v2-topologystatefilter"></a>
**Message `com.daml.ledger.api.v2.TopologyStateFilter`**

- Source: [community/ledger-api/src/main/protobuf/com/daml/ledger/api/v2/package_service.proto](https://github.com/DACH-NY/canton/blob/v3.4.11/community/ledger-api/src/main/protobuf/com/daml/ledger/api/v2/package_service.proto#L112)
- Fields: 2

Filter the vetted packages by the participant and synchronizer that they are
 hosted on.

 Empty fields are ignored, such that a ``TopologyStateFilter`` without
 participant_ids and without synchronizer_ids matches a vetted package hosted
 on any participant and synchronizer.

 Non-empty fields specify candidate values of which at least one must match.
 If both fields are set then at least one candidate value must match from each
 field.

| Field | Type | Label | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| participant_ids | `string` | repeated | If this list is non-empty, only vetted packages hosted on participants<br/> listed in this field match the filter.<br/> Query the current Ledger API's participant's ID via the public<br/> ``GetParticipantId`` command in ``PartyManagementService``. |
| synchronizer_ids | `string` | repeated | If this list is non-empty, only vetted packages from the topology state of<br/> the synchronizers in this list match the filter. |

<a id="type-com-daml-ledger-api-v2-topologytransaction"></a>
**Message `com.daml.ledger.api.v2.TopologyTransaction`**

- Source: [community/ledger-api/src/main/protobuf/com/daml/ledger/api/v2/topology_transaction.proto](https://github.com/DACH-NY/canton/blob/v3.4.11/community/ledger-api/src/main/protobuf/com/daml/ledger/api/v2/topology_transaction.proto#L16)
- Fields: 6

_No description._

| Field | Type | Label | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| update_id | `string` | optional | Assigned by the server. Useful for correlating logs.<br/> Must be a valid LedgerString (as described in ``value.proto``).<br/> Required |
| offset | `int64` | optional | The absolute offset. The details of this field are described in ``community/ledger-api/README.md``.<br/> Required, it is a valid absolute offset (positive integer). |
| synchronizer_id | `string` | optional | A valid synchronizer id.<br/> Identifies the synchronizer that synchronized the topology transaction.<br/> Required |
| record_time | `google.protobuf.Timestamp` | optional | The time at which the changes in the topology transaction become effective. There is a small delay between a<br/> topology transaction being sequenced and the changes it contains becoming effective. Topology transactions appear<br/> in order relative to a synchronizer based on their effective time rather than their sequencing time.<br/> Required |
| events | [`com.daml.ledger.api.v2.TopologyEvent`](#type-com-daml-ledger-api-v2-topologyevent) | repeated | A non-empty list of topology events.<br/> Required |
| trace_context | [`com.daml.ledger.api.v2.TraceContext`](#type-com-daml-ledger-api-v2-tracecontext) | optional | Optional; ledger API trace context<br/><br/> The trace context transported in this message corresponds to the trace context supplied<br/> by the client application in a HTTP2 header of the original command submission.<br/> We typically use a header to transfer this type of information. Here we use message<br/> body, because it is used in gRPC streams which do not support per message headers.<br/> This field will be populated with the trace context contained in the original submission.<br/> If that was not provided, a unique ledger-api-server generated trace context will be used<br/> instead. |

<a id="type-com-daml-ledger-api-v2-transaction"></a>
**Message `com.daml.ledger.api.v2.Transaction`**

- Source: [community/ledger-api/src/main/protobuf/com/daml/ledger/api/v2/transaction.proto](https://github.com/DACH-NY/canton/blob/v3.4.11/community/ledger-api/src/main/protobuf/com/daml/ledger/api/v2/transaction.proto#L17)
- Fields: 10

Filtered view of an on-ledger transaction's create and archive events.

| Field | Type | Label | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| update_id | `string` | optional | Assigned by the server. Useful for correlating logs.<br/> Must be a valid LedgerString (as described in ``value.proto``).<br/> Required |
| command_id | `string` | optional | The ID of the command which resulted in this transaction. Missing for everyone except the submitting party.<br/> Must be a valid LedgerString (as described in ``value.proto``).<br/> Optional |
| workflow_id | `string` | optional | The workflow ID used in command submission.<br/> Must be a valid LedgerString (as described in ``value.proto``).<br/> Optional |
| effective_at | `google.protobuf.Timestamp` | optional | Ledger effective time.<br/> Required |
| events | [`com.daml.ledger.api.v2.Event`](#type-com-daml-ledger-api-v2-event) | repeated | The collection of events.<br/> Contains:<br/><br/> - ``CreatedEvent`` or ``ArchivedEvent`` in case of ACS_DELTA transaction shape<br/> - ``CreatedEvent`` or ``ExercisedEvent`` in case of LEDGER_EFFECTS transaction shape<br/><br/> Required |
| offset | `int64` | optional | The absolute offset. The details of this field are described in ``community/ledger-api/README.md``.<br/> Required, it is a valid absolute offset (positive integer). |
| synchronizer_id | `string` | optional | A valid synchronizer id.<br/> Identifies the synchronizer that synchronized the transaction.<br/> Required |
| trace_context | [`com.daml.ledger.api.v2.TraceContext`](#type-com-daml-ledger-api-v2-tracecontext) | optional | Optional; ledger API trace context<br/><br/> The trace context transported in this message corresponds to the trace context supplied<br/> by the client application in a HTTP2 header of the original command submission.<br/> We typically use a header to transfer this type of information. Here we use message<br/> body, because it is used in gRPC streams which do not support per message headers.<br/> This field will be populated with the trace context contained in the original submission.<br/> If that was not provided, a unique ledger-api-server generated trace context will be used<br/> instead. |
| record_time | `google.protobuf.Timestamp` | optional | The time at which the transaction was recorded. The record time refers to the synchronizer<br/> which synchronized the transaction.<br/> Required |
| external_transaction_hash | `bytes` | optional | For transaction externally signed, contains the external transaction hash<br/> signed by the external party. Can be used to correlate an external submission with a committed transaction.<br/> Optional |

| reassignment_id | `string` | optional | The ID of the unassignment. This needs to be used as an input for a assign ReassignmentCommand.<br/> Must be a valid LedgerString (as described in ``value.proto``).<br/> Required |
| contract_id | `string` | optional | The ID of the reassigned contract.<br/> Must be a valid LedgerString (as described in ``value.proto``).<br/> Required |
| template_id | `com.daml.ledger.api.v2.Identifier` | optional | The template of the reassigned contract.<br/> The identifier uses the package-id reference format.<br/><br/> Required |
| source | `string` | optional | The ID of the source synchronizer<br/> Must be a valid synchronizer id<br/> Required |
| target | `string` | optional | The ID of the target synchronizer<br/> Must be a valid synchronizer id<br/> Required |
| submitter | `string` | optional | Party on whose behalf the unassign command was executed.<br/> Empty if the unassignment happened offline via the repair service.<br/> Must be a valid PartyIdString (as described in ``value.proto``).<br/> Optional |
| reassignment_counter | `uint64` | optional | Each corresponding assigned and unassigned event has the same reassignment_counter. This strictly increases<br/> with each unassign command for the same contract. Creation of the contract corresponds to reassignment_counter<br/> equals zero.<br/> Required |
| assignment_exclusivity | `google.protobuf.Timestamp` | optional | Assignment exclusivity<br/> Before this time (measured on the target synchronizer), only the submitter of the unassignment can initiate the assignment<br/> Defined for reassigning participants.<br/> Optional |
| witness_parties | `string` | repeated | The parties that are notified of this event.<br/> Required |
| package_name | `string` | optional | The package name of the contract.<br/> Required |
| offset | `int64` | optional | The offset of origin.<br/> Offsets are managed by the participant nodes.<br/> Reassignments can thus NOT be assumed to have the same offsets on different participant nodes.<br/> Required, it is a valid absolute offset (positive integer) |
| node_id | `int32` | optional | The position of this event in the originating reassignment.<br/> Node IDs are not necessarily equal across participants,<br/> as these may see different projections/parts of reassignments.<br/> Required, must be valid node ID (non-negative integer) |

<a id="type-com-daml-ledger-api-v2-updateformat"></a>
**Message `com.daml.ledger.api.v2.UpdateFormat`**

- Source: [community/ledger-api/src/main/protobuf/com/daml/ledger/api/v2/transaction_filter.proto](https://github.com/DACH-NY/canton/blob/v3.4.11/community/ledger-api/src/main/protobuf/com/daml/ledger/api/v2/transaction_filter.proto#L169)
- Fields: 3

A format specifying what updates to include and how to render them.

| Field | Type | Label | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| include_transactions | [`com.daml.ledger.api.v2.TransactionFormat`](#type-com-daml-ledger-api-v2-transactionformat) | optional | Include Daml transactions in streams.<br/> Optional, if unset, no transactions are emitted in the stream. |
| include_reassignments | [`com.daml.ledger.api.v2.EventFormat`](#type-com-daml-ledger-api-v2-eventformat) | optional | Include (un)assignments in the stream.<br/> The events in the result take the shape TRANSACTION_SHAPE_ACS_DELTA.<br/> Optional, if unset, no (un)assignments are emitted in the stream. |
| include_topology_events | [`com.daml.ledger.api.v2.TopologyFormat`](#type-com-daml-ledger-api-v2-topologyformat) | optional | Include topology events in streams.<br/> Optional, if unset no topology events are emitted in the stream. |

<a id="type-com-daml-ledger-api-v2-usermanagementfeature"></a>
**Message `com.daml.ledger.api.v2.UserManagementFeature`**

- Source: [community/ledger-api/src/main/protobuf/com/daml/ledger/api/v2/version_service.proto](https://github.com/DACH-NY/canton/blob/v3.4.11/community/ledger-api/src/main/protobuf/com/daml/ledger/api/v2/version_service.proto#L65)
- Fields: 3

_No description._

| Field | Type | Label | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| supported | `bool` | optional | Whether the Ledger API server provides the user management service. |
| max_rights_per_user | `int32` | optional | The maximum number of rights that can be assigned to a single user.<br/> Servers MUST support at least 100 rights per user.<br/> A value of 0 means that the server enforces no rights per user limit. |
| max_users_page_size | `int32` | optional | The maximum number of users the server can return in a single response (page).<br/> Servers MUST support at least a 100 users per page.<br/> A value of 0 means that the server enforces no page size limit. |

<a id="type-com-daml-ledger-api-v2-vettedpackage"></a>
**Message `com.daml.ledger.api.v2.VettedPackage`**

- Source: [community/ledger-api/src/main/protobuf/com/daml/ledger/api/v2/package_reference.proto](https://github.com/DACH-NY/canton/blob/v3.4.11/community/ledger-api/src/main/protobuf/com/daml/ledger/api/v2/package_reference.proto#L27)
- Fields: 5

A package that is vetting on a given participant and synchronizer,
 modelled after ``VettedPackage`` in `topology.proto &lt;https://github.com/digital-asset/canton/blob/main/community/base/src/main/protobuf/com/digitalasset/canton/protocol/v30/topology.proto#L206&gt;`_,
 enriched with the package name and version.

| Field | Type | Label | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| package_id | `string` | optional | Package ID of this package. Always present. |
| valid_from_inclusive | `google.protobuf.Timestamp` | optional | The time from which this package is vetted. Empty if vetting time has no<br/> lower bound. |
| valid_until_exclusive | `google.protobuf.Timestamp` | optional | The time until which this package is vetted. Empty if vetting time has no<br/> upper bound. |
| package_name | `string` | optional | Name of this package.<br/> Only available if the package has been uploaded to the current participant.<br/> If unavailable, is empty string. |
| package_version | `string` | optional | Version of this package.<br/> Only available if the package has been uploaded to the current participant.<br/> If unavailable, is empty string. |

<a id="type-com-daml-ledger-api-v2-vettedpackages"></a>
**Message `com.daml.ledger.api.v2.VettedPackages`**

- Source: [community/ledger-api/src/main/protobuf/com/daml/ledger/api/v2/package_reference.proto](https://github.com/DACH-NY/canton/blob/v3.4.11/community/ledger-api/src/main/protobuf/com/daml/ledger/api/v2/package_reference.proto#L54)
- Fields: 4

The list of packages vetted on a given participant and synchronizer, modelled
 after ``VettedPackages`` in `topology.proto &lt;https://github.com/digital-asset/canton/blob/main/community/base/src/main/protobuf/com/digitalasset/canton/protocol/v30/topology.proto#L206&gt;`_.
 The list only contains packages that matched a filter in the query that
 originated it.

| Field | Type | Label | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| packages | [`com.daml.ledger.api.v2.VettedPackage`](#type-com-daml-ledger-api-v2-vettedpackage) | repeated | Sorted by package_name and package_version where known, and package_id as a<br/> last resort. |
| participant_id | `string` | optional | Participant on which these packages are vetted. Always present. |
| synchronizer_id | `string` | optional | Synchronizer on which these packages are vetted. Always present. |
| topology_serial | `uint32` | optional | Serial of last ``VettedPackages`` topology transaction of this participant<br/> and on this synchronizer. Always present. |
